snippet:
Majestic Café is undeniably one of Porto’s most iconic spots. Known for its stunning Belle Époque architecture, elegant chandeliers, ornate mirrors, and rich wooden interiors, it really does feel like stepping back in time. Even if you don’t eat here, it’s worth visiting and getting a coffee just to sit inside and soak up the atmosphere. It’s a hugely popular tourist attraction, so expect it to be busy throughout the day.

The food and drinks were fairly average, and definitely on the overpriced side, though that’s somewhat expected given the café’s reputation and location. We ordered an espresso and a selection of tapas, which came to around €33, and while everything was perfectly fine, nothing particularly stood out flavour-wise. This is definitely more of a place you come for the experience and ambience rather than the food itself. Also limited options for those who are pure vegetarian.

Service was excellent throughout our visit, staff were friendly, professional, and helpful despite the constant crowds and queues.

There are queues pretty much all through the morning and afternoon, so I’d definitely recommend avoiding peak lunch hours if possible. I visited during an off-peak period and still had to queue, though it was manageable. I can imagine it becomes significantly busier during peak tourist season.
Weekdays are slightly calmer, but queues are still very much part of the experience.

Majestic Café is one of those places that’s absolutely worth experiencing at least once while in Porto. Go for the history, architecture, and atmosphere, not necessarily for a standout meal. A beautiful and iconic stop that captures the charm of old-world Porto perfectly.

snippet:
The cafe's long history (since 1922) and beautiful and ornate decoration are good reasons to pay a visit when you're in Porto. Prices here are probably more than double of what you would pay in a regular cafe, but you get to enjoy the classic ambience and atmosphere. Service is impeccable by waiters and waitresses elegantly dressed. The captain is dressed in a tuxedo! Service is professional and polite regardless what you order.
We had a vegetable soup and it's iconic french toast and some non-alcoholic drinks. Both the soup and french toast were very good.
We are glad we took the advice of our tour guide Andre from Buendia to visit Majestic Cafe. Great recommendation!

snippet:
Had a really lovely experience. We ordered the French toast, chocolate cake, croissant with butter, and cappuccinos — everything was delicious and beautifully presented.

The café was quite busy, but it never felt rushed or overwhelming, which made it easy to relax and enjoy the atmosphere. Service was excellent throughout — attentive, friendly, and efficient.

Definitely a spot worth visiting if you’re in Porto!

snippet: I was so surprised when we managed to find a table for our group of seven. Our waiter was extremely polite, the coffees arrived very quickly, and they were delicious. Both the indoor and outdoor architecture are pure art — truly marvelous and iconic. We spent a long time admiring every detail, from the walls and ceiling to the seating areas. The only slightly uncomfortable thing was the cold temperature inside, but aside from that, everything felt like an experience in itself.

snippet: This is the mother of tourist traps in Porto. Built in 1922, a lot of history there, classic, rustic, elegant decor, wait staff in white and black tuxedos, €50 for a tuna sandwich, an affogato coffee and a glass of local white wine.  They charge the same amount for the breakfast set.  If you want to visit here for the sake of checking in to a historical spot (like we did), it is all good. But do not come here for the medicore food and over prices drinks.  We got lucky when we got here around 1pm, no wait.  But there is usually a huge line outside for people to get ripped off.

snippet:
It's old it's charming and it's friendly. Has a very nice menu and an extensive wine list. I had the ham and cheese croissant with a glass of white wine and to be fair is probably the one the nicest ham and cheese croissants I've ever had. I certainly had a few.

It's like a trip to another era, the staff addressed in wholesale uniforms. The maitre d, is very friendly and accommodating.

The decor is what you'd expect to be, old style and very ornate.

snippet:
This place is not only a great spot for photos but also one where you can feel the essence of medieval European history. We ordered Majestic Café’s signature dessert, French Toast (Rabanadas Majestic Style) along with an ice cream selection and orange juice. The French toast has more of a moist, dessert-like texture, soaked in sweet syrup, rather than the typical style—making it an excellent choice when your energy levels are low.

The chandeliers, golden decorations, piano, and the elegant, intricate wooden details create a palace-like atmosphere. The mirrored walls also make the space feel much larger than it actually is. If you visit early in the morning, you can get a seat without waiting outside. The restroom is located in the basement.

snippet:
Is it pretty? Absolutely. Does it compensate for the food? Absolutely not.
I had a double espresso that costed €9 and a ham and cheese toast. The coffee was terrible, burnt over-extracted coffee grounds that left a disgusting bitter aftertaste. They filled my cup with water, which I didn’t ask for, and also is NOT how you serve it. The “toast” was not completely toasted, it was cold and mushy on the inside. They clearly only cared about how it looked outside, which explains my opening line.
My friend had a cappuccino that was also very mediocre. The only thing I would recommend are the scones.
Service is great, in fact is the only reason why I decided to try it because prices are exorbitant. Also they have a beautiful piano in the room but for some reason the person supposedly playing it was only guiding customers to the restrooms??
I do not recommend this place.

snippet:
We visited Majestic Café in Porto last week, and honestly, if you enjoy being overcharged, this is the place for you. We paid €15 for one black coffee and one cappuccino, which would have been acceptable if the quality and service matched the price — unfortunately, they did not.

The black coffee was served with boiling hot milk on the side without even asking whether we preferred hot or cold milk. A more attentive or experienced waiter would have checked this with the customer. The cappuccino was also made with excessively hot milk, to the point that the milk tasted burnt.

The service was average at best. If I’m paying €15 for two coffees, I expect much better attention to detail. Even simple things were lacking: the sugar packet was carelessly placed beside the cup, and the paper napkin was not properly arranged under the milk jar.

The décor and atmosphere are beautiful, so it may still be worth visiting for the interior alone. However, when it comes to the quality of the drinks and the overall experience, it simply isn’t worth the price.

On a scale from 1 to 5, I would give it a 1 — or even a 0 if possible.

The black coffee was served with boiling hot milk on the side without even asking whether we preferred hot or cold milk. A more attentive or experienced waiter would have checked this with the customer. The cappuccino was also made with excessively hot milk, to the point that the milk tasted burnt.

The service was average at best. If I’m paying €15 for two coffees, I expect much better attention to detail. Even simple things were lacking: the sugar packet was carelessly placed beside the cup, and the paper napkin was not properly arranged under the milk jar.

The décor and atmosphere are beautiful, so it may still be worth visiting for the interior alone. However, when it comes to the quality of the drinks and the overall experience, it simply isn’t worth the price.

On a scale from 1 to 5, I would give it a 1 — or even a 0 if possible.'}

snippet: Lovely place and definitely a must see in Porto. The interior and exterior are stunning and truly transport you back to the 1920s. That said, the food did not meet even mid-level expectations, especially considering the prices. I ordered the francesinha, which arrived cold and it was just acceptable at best, leaning more toward disappointing. Overall, worth visiting for the atmosphere, but not for the food.

snippet: We stopped by Majestic Café late in the evening for drinks and dessert, and it turned out to be the perfect time to visit. Because it wasn’t completely packed, we could truly soak in the nostalgia and grand history of the space without the usual daytime crowds. Our waitress was attentive, and while the dessert we shared was just okay, the drinks and the atmosphere made up for it. It was a wonderful, relaxed visit—just sitting back, absorbing the stunning interior, and enjoying the timeless vibe.

snippet: Very disappointed. I paid €9 for a coffee and €3 for pastel de nata! Never paid such a high price for a coffee in my life, in any other country I have been! In Porto, a coffee is usually around €1 and a pastel de nata about €1.50. Of course, I wasn’t expecting so little since it’s a nice place, but I definitely wasn’t expecting €12. There were no visible prices on the menu, and it honestly felt like I was charged more because I was a tourist. Beware tourists, this experience really left a bitter taste in my mouth and it’s definitely not worth it.

snippet: Is this the best cup of coffee or the most affordable snack you’ll find in Porto? Probably not. But is it an absolute must-do experience? Without a doubt. Walking into Majestic Café is like stepping back into the Belle Époque; the ornate wood carvings, gilded mirrors, and marble creates an atmosphere you just can’t find anywhere else. Go for the stunning architecture and the history, enjoy the elegance of the service, and consider the 'tourist premium' a fair price for the incredible surroundings.

snippet:
On the expensive end for sure, but considering you can sit for a long while cradling the same drink and pastery, what you're really paying for is the ambiance. It's gorgeous, hard to compare to other restaurants and cafes.

One thing that is a must is the French toast, unlike any I've had before it easily takes the cake so to speak for the best. Another memorable menu item was the hot chocolate, with it's thickness highly reccomend getting it with the whipped cream to help cut some of the richness.

snippet:
The cafe is quite amazing - seemingly the original decor lovingly kept since 1924. The food was excellent, and atmosphere is lovely and lively!  It’s highly popular with the tourists.
My only issue was that the head waiter was overly friendly, and felt the need to touch everyone.  He was a very nice and sincere man, but honestly it was a little strange he touched everyone.  If you get a female server you will be fine - lol.
The prices reflected the tourists - with the “Belle epoch” breakfast at €50 per person. Honestly a bit over the top. You can have a full dinner at a number of excellent restos in Porto for that price.

snippet:
I think this is neither worth the hype nor the prices.
Pros:
- Able to get a table for 4 right away
- Pretty
- Multilingual staff
- Fast service
Cons:
- You're stuffed in like sardines (pun fully intended)
- The prices are truly stupid. 9 Euros for a double espresso? Wild.
- For the grandeur of the dining room, the bathrooms are pretty disgusting. Immediate wall of smell, dingy, and gross.

Ultimately, we're happy we went... but likely wouldn't recommend.

snippet:
I had dinner at the Majestic Café in Porto. The café is unbelievably gorgeous — both inside and out — and truly worth seeing. Unfortunately, the service was quite disappointing. I ordered a Francesinha, which was just okay, nothing special. The meal was also very expensive for what it was.

Overall, I’d recommend visiting the Majestic Café to admire its stunning architecture and atmosphere, but not for the food. It’s perfect for taking pictures, less so for dining.

snippet:
Majestic Café is definitely not as good as its reputation suggests. The coffee is quite bitter and, at €6, it is not worth the price at all. The interior is very cramped, so it’s not comfortable to sit and spend time there. The staff were also not welcoming at all; there was no friendliness or basic hospitality, not even a simple ‘enjoy your meal’.
The Pastel de Nata was also the worst I had in Porto; it tasted stale, overly vanilla-flavored, and had a thick pastry.
Overall, it doesn’t deserve the hype, and I would not recommend it.

snippet: Very upscale and a photo stop for many tourists. I sat outside and had a glass of sparkling wine, no food. No less than 10 people stopped to get their photo taken while I was sitting there. I love it though! Service was kind and considerate, yet slow. Under the circumstances of being such a hot spot, it was to be expected. I have yet to have the elusive Porto Francesinha sandwich. I call it elusive because either I am not hungry enough to order it, or the places I have gone haven't had it.  They have it on their menu, but I wasn't hungry enough to order. I have less than 24 hours here in Porto, so fingers crossed that I can enjoy the famous sandwich before I leave.

snippet: We totally knew what we were getting into with this TOURIST cafe - we knew it would be a wait, and expensive, because of the tourist nature of it. However, that was fine - we were on vacation and it really was quite pretty inside.  We actually had a lovely time waiting in line outside in the queue, in fact, chit chatting with other tourists. I think we might have waited about 30 mins on a Sunday late morning. It was a photo oppo for sure, but the food was good too. We only had some tea/coffee and dessert items, but it was served quickly, attractively and friendly and it tasted good.  It was a "one and done" for us, but I'm still glad we went for a snack.

snippet:
18.12.2024
Visiting Majestic Café in Porto was one of the most memorable experiences of my trip to Portugal. From the very first step inside, I felt as if I had been transported back in time. The café is breathtakingly beautiful: high ceilings, elegant chandeliers, large mirrors, and exquisite wooden details — all creating an incredible old-world atmosphere. This is not just a café, it is living history.
I ordered a cappuccino and the famous Portuguese pastel de nata. The coffee was rich and well-balanced, while the pastry was warm, crispy, and delicate. The service was excellent: the staff were attentive and friendly, yet never intrusive.
What impressed me the most was the atmosphere. Sitting at a table, it’s easy to imagine writers, artists, and thinkers once gathering here. This is not just a place to have coffee, it’s a cultural experience and a chance to connect with the soul of Porto.

Yes, the prices are higher than in an ordinary café, but here you are paying not only for food and drinks, but also for the unique ambiance, history, and the privilege of being part of this heritage. For me, it was absolutely worth it.

Majestic Café is not just a coffee stop — it’s a memory that will stay with me forever.

— Chris, USA 🇺🇸

snippet:
I had the opportunity to visit Café Majestic during my recent trip to Porto, and I must say, it is truly a place that leaves an impression from the very first moment. The venue is stunning, with beautiful Belle Époque architecture and a timeless ambiance that transports you back to the early 20th century. The Art Nouveau interior, the antique mirrors, and the ornate details create a refined and visually remarkable atmosphere. It’s clear why this café has become a must-see attraction for so many visitors.

I ordered the torrija, and it was absolutely delicious. I highly recommend trying it, ideally paired with a coffee or a glass of Port wine—it’s a truly enjoyable combination. The texture was perfect, and the balance of sweetness and spice, along with the elegant presentation, made it a highlight of my visit.

That being said, the prices are quite high compared to other places in Porto. It’s evident that part of what you’re paying for is the historical charm and iconic status of the venue. The atmosphere is very touristy, so if you’re seeking a more local or casual experience, this may not be the ideal choice.

Overall, I suggest visiting Café Majestic at least once to appreciate the historic setting and to indulge in the delightful torrija—a treat well worth the stop.

Otherwise stand outside take your pictures and move 10 meters down the road and feed a family of 4 with breakfast for 25 euro total.

snippet:
The cafés in Portugal have, up to now, been exclusively the hot bed of revolutionaries, the business meeting point of merchant groups, or where the obstinate aged listlessly passed the time.
An example of what a cafe should actually be, however, has just been given to us. We are referring to the newest establishment of this modern type of cafe that was just inaugurated at one of the important central points of Oporto, the entrance of the Rua de Santa Catarina. It is one of the most noble and luxurious cafes that we know of, for which its name Majestic is most suited and justified. Ladies from the best of Oporto’s society frequent it and here we have a perfect example of the new and pleasant role of the cafe in our country.

Way too much

snippet: The special thing about this restaurant may not be about the food. What had interested me was the atmosphere.  People gathered at the front door, and most of them were just wanting to take a glance of the insides, with a few shots  of photos. A smart waiter welcomes and arranges  seats in a good and humorous manner. Speed of services is super, reminding me of my home country. The texture of the furniture is lovely to be touched and felt. People around are chatting lively, sometimes with some laughing or crying from babies. As said, there are quite a lot of interesting things to enjoy.

snippet:
Great spot for lunch.  Stopped in around 1pm during the week and it was easy to grab a table.  There was a doorman to great and seat us, which was a nice touch.

Ordered the Francesinha sandwich, which is a Porto must.  Delicious, tender meat with toasted bread covered in melted cheese and sitting in a gravy type sauce.  Wonderful!!

The fries were irregular pieces of potato, with some crispy and others soft and puffy.  We shared them, each eating the ones we liked more.

Service was attentive, but not overbearing.  Remember to ask for your check when you're done or you'll sit there undisturbed.

Would 100% go back.

snippet: Very lovely interior with interesting history.  A fun place to sit down for a bite but overall it was more  “tourist trap” than “good meal”.  We ordered the French toast, which came with a single slice for 9€ and while it was good, the portion just didn’t make sense.  The cheesecake was also fine, but completely forgettable.  Glad to say we went there just to say it, but if given the chance, I’d simply step inside and take a photo, and leave it at that. I’d also note that the menu is fairly limited so we settled on sweet treats as we arrived later in the evening.

snippet:
This is the most expensive and not tasty cafe for this money.
The meat for 36 euros is tough. Although the sauce is tasty, sweet.
The chocolate cake is soaked and moist but ..too sweet.
And the special waffles are: a piece of bread heavily fried in honey, a very fatty dessert. Really do not order it!
The coffee is ordinary, too high price for simple coffee.
Dirty plates and cups on the table were not removed from the table until the very end, the employees, although in suits, do not monitor the cleanliness of the table.
Does not justify the time spent in the queue.
But yes, the place is beautiful 😅

snippet:
"Beauty is only skin deep". In summary - Avoid.

The place looks pretty fancy from the outside but in my opinion it looked a little unkempt and dated.

The coffee was atrocious - I've had better free coffee while a patient in hospital! And the americano cost €6 - this is borderline criminal (akin to St Marks square in Venice where it costs 15 euro for an espresso during the masked festival! At least that one tasted nice...).

Barely gets 2 stars from me - waiting staff weren't the kindest, but the decor is worth a brief visit just to see. I wouldn't order anything else from here, and won't be coming again or recommending others to actually order anything other than maybe a water.

The Imperial McDonalds looks cleaner and had a nicer ambience.

First I had burger and fruit juice. They did not ask the level of doneness.  The burger was okay. Then I wanted to try dessert and ordered an apple tart with cinnamon. They did not change my paper placemat which was quite dirty. That is something you would not expect from a high-end restaurant. The dessert is also neither good nor bad. The ambiance was not bad. The piank performance was a nice touch.

I wouldn't recommend. Hopefully things improve in the future.

snippet: Majestic Cafe, which is definitely on the list of those who visit Porto, is a must-see cafe with its ambiance and its origins date back to 1922. Torrijas (French Toast - €8) is the signature dish of this place. Cheesecake w Red Fruits (€8) also conquered hearts. Regarding time selection, if you go after 11:00 you will have to wait at the door for a while. The elegance of the place, the kindness of the service staff and the historical ambience make this place one of the must-see addresses, apart from the food. We tried Nata here, as in every place we went. If you pass, you don’t lose anything.

snippet:
An iconic but overpriced experience in Porto! The Majestic Cafe certainly lives up to its name with its stunning Belle Époque interior, but prepare your wallet for the premium you'll pay for the atmosphere. 💸

The french toast (rabanadas) was absolutely divine - crispy exterior, custardy inside, and perfectly sweet. Definitely the highlight of my visit and worth trying if you go.

However, the Francesinha was a major disappointment. Even with a high salt tolerance, it was overwhelmingly salty to the point of being inedible. For a traditional Porto dish, this was a letdown at such a renowned establishment.

The cheesecake fell somewhere in the middle - decent but nothing memorable or worth the hefty price tag. Just your standard cheesecake that you could find at many other cafes for significantly less.

While the historic ambiance and architecture are undeniably beautiful, the inconsistent food quality and eye-watering prices make this more of a "one-time experience" than a place to regularly enjoy. If you visit, stick to coffee and the french toast, and maybe just take photos of the gorgeous interior.

3/5 - Worth one visit for the experience and the french toast, but temper your expectations for the rest.

snippet:
Visiting Café Majestic was a truly memorable experience. The atmosphere is absolutely enchanting — it feels like stepping back in time, with elegant decor and a unique historic charm that sets it apart from any other place in Porto. The service was excellent: attentive, professional, and very friendly, which made the visit even more enjoyable.

That said, I did find the prices to be quite high. While it might not be the most budget-friendly spot, I understand that part of what you’re paying for is the history and ambiance of this iconic café. For those who appreciate cultural heritage and a special setting, it may well be worth it.

All in all, it’s a beautiful place to visit at least once — for the experience, the history, and a taste of something truly classic.

snippet:
We heard a lot about this place and had some expectations about it. The food was both basic and expensive and by basic I am not pretending to exaggerate but when you see on the menu a 20 Euro sandwich or a 12
Euro Croissant you expect at least something that’s worth the price. 45 Euro for a breakfast menu with a tiny orange juice, basic scrambled eggs and a basket with bread plus a small cup of chocolate. It’s like paying entrance to a museum that includes a snack because the place is beautiful but just to take pictures and then go someplace else to have real food.

snippet: Beautiful art noeveau cafe with gained notoriety of JK Rowling having been rumored to have written the 1st 3 chapters of Harry Potter's Sorcerer's Stone here. The standout for us was Helena C. who in spite of having to deal with so many tourists in and out - she was a saint! So delightful and friendly and recommended such lovely and insightful cocktails to us. We had a lovely drink during the day and the following evening we had just finished dinner and happened to walk by again and was so surprised it was still open at 10pm! It turns out they are open till 11pm sometimes, please check. It was more enjoyable with so much less people at night and this time we sat where JK Rowling sat which is near to the piano. To our wonderful surprise Helena our favorite person happen to be working and she recommended the french toast with the orange sauce and to pair it with their port wine; but when she sensed my hesitation she brought me a sample taste and it turned out I don't really like the port so I ordered my favorite cocktail instead from the day before. Seriously best french toast ever, not too sweet . It was enchanting in the evening and we even noticed the courtyard in the back and focused more on the light fixtures this time and other details.

The team is just the kindest, most warmest, and most accommodating people you will meet ever. Never once a No! Every request met with the warmest smiles ever. The ambiance ofcourse is beautiful and like stepping back in time. Chic, elegant and charming! We started with breakfast and left post lunch that is how much we loved it here. snippet: We came here because of the link with JK Rowling and Harry Potter. Atmosphere was nice - you can see how it might have inspired the books. Service was excellent and very friendly. Food and drink is very expensive (e.g. 7 euros for a coffee, 9 euros for a cake) but we were expecting this. The food is ok but nothing exceptional. My overall summary would be come here for the vibe and experience, but don’t come here if it’s just for the food and drink as it would be too expensive otherwise.

snippet:
Stumbling on the streets of Porto, you find something that pops out in a unique way - it’s kind of finding a scrap of very old newspaper that’s vintage and talks an important part of history. This place is like no other. This place is special. We’re talking about a “Grand Cafe”, something that truly reflects what a cafe should be.
At the door, you’ll find the Manager who’s on top of this business, well dressed and maintains one of the most charming body language I’ve ever seen someone professionally pulling it off. He’s got the Gatsby smile, and takes care of you in a way that suggests: what took you so long, old friend?
The place is superbly decorated, kind of like something extracted from Titanic dine rooms. It’s a place where you’d like to compliment your rendezvous with formal attire.
The seats, benches, ceiling and tables are all designed and decorated for you to stay and enjoy a place you don’t find quite often. It resembles remarkable aesthetics accompanied with notable designs and artwork.
You’ll also have a nice cup of coffee, and an outstanding french toast. Why are you reading this? JUST GO!

We got there around 10am in the morning and there was no line. That was nice.

snippet:
Cold, Overpriced, and Not as Promised

Unfortunately, this coffee shop was a disappointment. During winter, there was no heating, and it was actually colder inside than outside (around 8°C). While the service was acceptable, it did not compensate for the overall experience.

The prices are extremely high, comparable to a 5-star “Leading Hotels of the World” level:
Espresso: €6
Cappuccino: €8
However, the atmosphere is nothing special and does not justify these prices. I’m not sure why this place is so highly recommended.

In Porto, you can find many traditional cafés with friendly people, a warm atmosphere, and a great selection of food and drinks at much more reasonable prices. I would strongly recommend choosing one of those instead.

Paid 21 euros for a white tea, croissant with jam, and a hot chocolate—one of the most expensive I’ve had. The croissant wasn’t impressive either—too mushy for my taste, and I’ve had much better for a lower price. Nice for a one-time visit, but not worth returning.

snippet: The pianist was very kind and talented and music was great overall, yet it was very crowded and it was impossible to hear the music from all of the noise. We ordered the Majestic Burger, Chocolate Cake and as wine ordered Porto Tawny. The wine was very good, had great smell and quality, however the burger was bland and was simply ordinary. The fries that came with the burger were good. The chocolate cake was also not that good, there are other places that are way better in terms of dessert. However, the waiters are attentive and very kind, and the interior design is impressive and perfect for photos. A nice place to catch a break, but not exactly a good food spot.

snippet:
Breakfast at the Majestic Cafe

Last time I had my coffee here about 10 years ago. And and I decided to visit to Majestic Café in Porto during my current trip, as it was an unforgettable experience last time
I'd like to highlight five positive points that stood out:

1. Exquisite Ambiance: The café's exterior and interior is a feast for the eyes with its Art Nouveau architecture, ornate mirrors, and beautifully carved woodwork. 🖼️ Stepping inside feels like traveling back in Time Machine to the early 20th century.

2. Impeccable Service: I think the very same guy was serving our table 10 years ago - very polite and charismatic. ))) The staff at Majestic Café are not only professional but also incredibly attentive and friendly. He really allowed me to feel welcomed and well-cared for, making my visit even more enjoyable.

3. Delightful Cuisine: The menu offers a delectable selection of mainly European dishes. I suggest the perfectly brewed coffee and - if you’re hungry - the fresh pastries and hearty meals.

4. Rich History. Since my visit this café already celebrated its hundreds anniversary. ❗️Majestic Café is steeped in history, having been a popular meeting place for writers, artists, and intellectuals and - of course tourists like me - since its opening in 1922. This rich heritage adds a unique charm and depth to the dining experience.

5. Cultural Hub. I love its selection of music, which creates nice atmosphere for morning coffee. Beyond its role as a café, Majestic serves as a cultural landmark in Porto.

Overall, Majestic Café is a must-visit for anyone in Porto, offering an unparalleled combination of history, culture, and culinary excellence.

snippet:
Ok…hands down the #1 best Pastel de Nata in Porto! Look no further I swear … flakey buttery crust, sweet, delicious creme with layers of flavor. Complex and delicious, perfect with a cafe con leite. Truly remarkable.
We then ordered a French Toast- all the reviews on Google rave about the French toast but I’m telling you it was too much; too sweet, unnecessary amount of sauce, and honestly it tasted like a fried, oily piece of bread either way a bunch of undetectable syrupy sauce all over- was it orange or lemon? And why is the cinnamon on the plate and not on the toast?
Skip the French toast and order two pastel de nata! You will thank me later!
I gave them 5 stars for food because the consistency was impressive- every French toast came out looking perfect and exactly the same size. Nd placement on the plate, etc. We only went for breakfast so I cannot comment on the other food but this is clearly a high quality establishment.
Wait staff were pleasant and helpful, and of course the architecture and design of the place was beautiful except for the bathroom which was hugely disappointing! A bathroom in a place like this deserves to be beautiful and befitting of the history and status of this establishment! This beautiful fine restaurant and tea room had a dismal dark bathroom that smelled of urine and had industrialized soap. (Not cool since Porto is famous for their beautiful soaps!) Very disappointing! (That’s why the lower mark for atmosphere). There was a lovely outdoor space for seating which looked modern but it was a bit rainy that day and no one was out there.

snippet:
This is one of those MUST GO places if you are visiting Porto, builded on the Belle époque era, you will feel transported to the past in this very impressive restaurant and cafe which tells a lot of Porto’s history for being the place where artists, writers and influence people got together during important historic’s moments of the city. The art and hand crafted work in this building is beyond espectacular. They offer a large menu where to choose, you can enjoy from a simple coffee to a large meal with family and friends. Majestic Cafe is always busy, it’s normal to see a line on the street waiting to go in, they have a very organized system so the line goes fast, we didn’t wait to long to get our table. Prices are not the cheapest but the experience is worth it. I totally recommend it!
Very professional staff. Service and assistant dogs are allowed.

snippet:
We stopped here for breakfast on our second day in Porto, and it definitely the cutest cafe we’ve been to in terms of historical-ness. Felt like we had taken a step back in time. Loved the architecture and cute tea sets.

The service was very nice, and although we didn’t have actual food (just had dessert) and tea, the experience was definitely very cute.

snippet:
I don't understand why anyone would pay 8€ for a subpar cappuccino when there are great affordable places nearby.
This place is a tourist trap if I've ever seen one. It's not so beautiful you can't skip it, it's packed with people, the staff clearly dislike being there and you pay the highest price I've ever seen for coffee and food that is worth a fraction of that.

It doesn't deserve the 4.1 at all.

The host tossed a sheet of placemat, two forks, and one spoon in front of us without proper explanation or setting. I consider this café is an upscale restaurant.  If there was a negative star, I would grade -5.  The service is cold and unwelcome.  Don’t spend $$$ here.  Go to other places and they will treat you better.

snippet: The food is so overpriced and overrated. Came here expecting food to be pricy, but we were far beyond disappointed. We ordered Pastel de Nata and it came here cold like it was left on the counter for days to rot. Literally we got Natas from elsewhere for 3 times less the price and still tasted better after heating it up in the microwave. We ordered an omlette that was so dry and bland, the salad vegetable sides were just pathetic, not to mention the soggy fries that I didn't even bother eating. I was just shocked cuz when i read other reviews people said its amazing, bla bla bla, its just not. period. The €6 darjeeling tea was just from TWG, in which I could've gotten a whole box with that price. It was just ridiculous. Food is mediocre at best, do not recommend.

snippet: I wouldn’t say i loved the place. We went there just because people are so hyped about it. They say it’s one of the most beautiful cafe in Europe, i can only somewhat agree (i mean look at those mirror in the cafe that is already rusty and overcrowded table placement, i can’t say it’s one of the most beautiful cafe in the Europe!). What worse was the french toast, i was really disappointed! It was not even hot and the toast to greasy and only has one taste, sweet. The tea selection was just regular TWG but cost 6€. Ice tea was bad, just like pour out of Arizona ice tea with a slice of lemon, the glass for soft drinks were just in bad condition. I don’t know how this cafe has over 4stars review. Staffs were just ok, not friendly but not bad or anything. And one last thing, it’s way overpriced

snippet: Don't GO this place !! this place is a total trap.  nothing special, just beautiful architecture.  What is referred to as coffee on the menu and costs 3 Euros is just an espresso.  If you want regular American coffee, double espresso comes in 5.50 Euros.  A Cafe who doesn't understand with coffee making. Terrifying !!  Also, there isn't even such a thing as a double espresso on the menu.  Espresso costs between 0.70 cents and 1.5 euros in every cafes and here is just 3 euros!!!.  The chocolate cake is overly sweet and devoid of flavor. I am still angry that i paid 16 Euros only for two double espresso and 1 chocolate cake !!!

snippet: We walked in and were pointed to a table right when you enter, directly in front of the door. There was another table, so close that the man's elbow from the other table was in my lap when I sat down.  We turned our table 45' so we had more space. The server told us we could not do that - but I am not going to eat with another person - whom I don't know! - infringing on my body while eating.  So we left.  Tourism gone wrong.  Simply pack everyone in - so they can say they ate there?? Not worth it for us.  We surely found good food in more comfortable space without strangers having body parts on us.

snippet:
On était content d'y aller et on s'attendait à des prix élevés (5€ la canette de Coca pour situer) mais ça va encore c'est les même prix que sur Paris donc pas étonné. Mais déjà le serveur principal était chelou il posait vachement ses mains sur nous, ce qui m'a déplu, puis le bouquet final c'est quand même que j'ai du demander 3 fois l'addition et attendre plus de 45min pour payer...
Donc ok on paie le cadre mais parfois faut savoir doser, donc je ne recommande pas, c'est excessif et un café local sera bien plus accueillant et à l'écoute de ses clients.

snippet:
No volvería a este sitio, el local bonito, (tampoco de los más bonitos a los que he ido), pero al final, lo ves "anunciado" por todos lados y quieres ir a verlo como buen turista.
Pues bien, fuimos a desayunar, ya sabiendo que no iba a ser barato, eso lo teníamos claro, pero lo que no teníamos claro es, que ya que íbamos a pagar una cantidad elevada por productos normales, el personal iba a ser tan desagradable y el ambiente del sitio tan malo. Las mesas pegadísimas unas a otras, tanto, que acabamos hablando con los de al lado y parecía que íbamos juntos, la calidad de la comida, regulinchi tirando a meehh... Un croasán con jamón y queso que se supone que es a la plancha, el relleno estaba frio, pero frio de recién sacado del frigo, no le llegó el calor ni de casualidad... Nos trajeron la comida antes que la bebida, para que se acabara de enfriar, mi amiga pidió un té y una torrija y claro, cuando llegó el té, que tienes que dejarlo en reposo, la torrija se enfrió...
En fin, que no aconsejo para nada ir a este local, estoy segura de que hay sitios mejores en la ciudad para un buen desayuno.

snippet:
Uno specchietto per le allodole.
Possiamo essere d’accordo che l’architettura interna al locale è interessante e attrattiva, ma se parliamo di cibo e servizio non ci siamo.
Abbiamo pagato una colazione con due cappuccini, due Pastel de nata e un toast, esattamente 37€.
Cappuccini di pessima qualità, impossibile berli, totalmente bruciati. Pastel de nata, forse del giorno prima?
Con la crema poco cotta e la sfoglia durissima.
Servizio lento.
Per quanto possa sembrare un posto allettante in cui accedervi per godersi un caffè, sconsiglio vivamente di farlo.

snippet: Não é por entrar num espaço antiquado como a sala de cerimónias do Titanic, com espelhos amassados ​​e desgastados pelo tempo, que se justificam os preços da carta. Como apreciador de boa gastronomia, conhecendo os preços das matérias-primas, sobretudo as de qualidade, e dos produtos requintados, o preço de um simples café a 6 euros é injustificável. Consigo compreender na Riviera Francesa, em Amalfi, em centros históricos como o Coliseu em Roma ou a Piazza San Marco em Veneza, ou em Miami, ou sob a tocha da Estátua da Liberdade. Mas, por favor, baixem um pouco os preços. Não sei como as pessoas conseguem pedir comida apesar dos preços exorbitantes. Suspeito que nunca tenham viajado, porque não tenho qualquer razão válida para justificar estes preços, nem a localização, nem o local onde se situa o restaurante, nem a ementa. Não me venham contar essa história de que as matérias-primas utilizadas são abusivas. Simplesmente ridículo.

Piège à touristes.
Nous avons pris le menu et avons payé un total de 90€. Le jus d’orange n’est pas pressé, café avec beaucoup trop de bulles, les plats sont très normaux. Pour un café populaire，la qualité des produits par rapport au prix n’est pas bon du tout.

snippet:
봄봄라떼랑 핫초콜릿, 나파를 먹었는데 봄봄라떼는 너무 달아서 머리가 아프고, 핫초콜릿은 거의 초콜릿 죽 수준이었습니다. 나파는 호텔 조식에 나오는 나파보다도 못했어요. 총평은 처참합니다. 내부는 예쁘지만, 예쁜 카페는 다른 곳에도 많습니다. 괜히 줄 서서 시간 낭비하지 마세요.

I tried the Spring Latte, Hot Chocolate, and Nappa. The Spring Latte was so sweet it gave me a headache, and the Hot Chocolate was more like chocolate porridge. The Nappa wasn’t even as good as what you get at a hotel breakfast. Overall, it was disappointing. The interior is pretty, but there are plenty of prettier cafés out there. Don’t waste your time waiting in line.

snippet:
價格昂貴，一片法式吐司就要8歐元，味道也太甜。巧克力蛋糕倒是很好吃，可以試試看。
店內裝潢氣氛不錯，給予小小肯定。
排隊的方法混亂，不斷有人擠進店中，店員也不會做管理。
服務速度很慢，從我們與店員要求結帳後至少等了半小時，依舊沒有獲得協助，等太久了沒事做所以來寫寫評論🐒
我能理解人很多服務生會很忙，但如果忙不過來的話是不是該考慮控管一下新客人進店的速度

10分鐘後的更新
服務生有各自服務的一側，所以找到其他服務生也沒用喔😇帳單終於來了，來看多久之後才能順利結帳🤩🤩

review_text_clean: 價格昂貴，一片法式吐司就要8歐元，味道也太甜。巧克力蛋糕倒是很好吃，可以試試看。 店內裝潢氣氛不錯，給予小小肯定。 排隊的方法混亂，不斷有人擠進店中，店員也不會做管理。 服務速度很慢，從我們與店員要求結帳後至少等了半小時，依舊沒有獲得協助，等太久了沒事做所以來寫寫評論🐒 我能理解人很多服務生會很忙，但如果忙不過來的話是不是該考慮控管一下新客人進店的速度 10分鐘後的更新 服務生有各自服務的一側，所以找到其他服務生也沒用喔😇帳單終於來了，來看多久之後才能順利結帳🤩🤩

snippet: Exageradamente caro. Hace años era un café donde poder tomar algo relajado disfrutando del local, pero ahora mismo es una trampa para turistas. Mesas muy juntas, colas para entrar en temporada alta y un precio desorbitado. 5€ el café pequeño (Abril 2019), que en cualquier cafetería cara de la ciudad no pasa de 1€. Como se puede comprobar con las fotos de la carta que hay por internet, han ido subiendo sin control el precio de todo. El mobiliario/suelos/lámparas no son originales, el local ha sido restaurado en 1994 en base a su diseño de 1921. El café está servido de manera exquisita, pero para nada justifica pagar 5 veces más que en cualquier sitio de la ciudad.

snippet:
Un sitio emblemático en Oporto... Emblema de cobrar una auténtica barbaridad por algo que no lo vale.
Un café cortado 5 euros, es un robo lo mires por donde lo mires.
El local es muy "bonito", demasiado rococó, pero para gustos los colores. El personal muy bien, y el café bueno (solo faltaba).
Hazme caso, entra, siéntate, levántate, haz unas fotos y vete.

snippet:
El sitio muy bonito. Lo demás, todo mal.

1. Nada más llegar, el camarero de gafas te atiende con una cara de mala hostia que se te quitan las ganas de entrar.
2. Los cafes, que aunque si bien los precios están fuera y sabíamos a lo que entrábamos, almenos podrian haber sido algo más. Un simple espresso y un simple cortado, ni presentación, ni adornos... Nada. Y además los hay mejores en cualquier otra cafeteria de Oporto. 12€ la broma.
3. Espacios entre mesas muy justos, bastante incómodo
4. Para pagar, no nos hacian caso. Tuvimos que pedir la cuenta hasta 3 veces. Y cuando nos la traen, tuvimos que pedir otras 2 veces que queríamos pagar con tarjeta, solo uno de los camareros llevaba datáfono. Hasta que no hicimos el amago de irnos, no aparecieron.

No recomiendo este sitio en absoluto. Una pareja se ha sentado a nuestro lado, y al ver como eran los cafés que teníamos y por los que iban a pagar también 12€, se han ido sin pedir nada. De verdad, para hacerse la foto vale, pero para todo lo demás, nada.

De verdad, para hacerse la foto vale, pero para todo lo demás, nada.', 'translated': "The place is very nice. Everything else is bad.

1. As soon as we arrived, the waiter with glasses greeted us with such a sour face that it made us not want to go in.

2. The coffees, although the prices were displayed outside and we knew what we were getting into, could at least have been better. A simple espresso and a simple cortado, no presentation, no garnish... Nothing. And you can find better coffees in any other café in Porto. €12 for the joke.

3. The tables were very cramped, quite uncomfortable.
4. When it came to paying, they ignored us. We had to ask for the bill three times. And when they finally brought it, we had to ask twice more that we wanted to pay by card; only one of the waiters had a card reader. They didn't appear until we pretended to leave.

I absolutely do not recommend this place. A couple sat down next to us, and when they saw the coffees we had and were going to pay €12 for them, they left without ordering anything. Honestly, it's fine for taking a picture, but for everything else, it's useless."}

Tenia muy altas expectativas sobre este restaurant, fui a tomar un café con mi esposa y todo estuvo mal, el café muy malo, muy elegante el restaurant pero no saben servir un buen café ni hacer latte art, el pastel de nata se sentía viejo y el cheesecake dejaba mucho que desear.

Sus precios demasiados altos para el servicio que ofrecen. Nada recomendado

snippet:
Un lugar precioso y con muchísima historia, ya que lo han conservado con la misma decoración y mobiliario que el día de su inauguración, el 17 de diciembre de 1921.
El motivo de haberlo puntuado tan bajo es porque me parece un lugar exageradamente caro. Un simple café tiene un precio de 5€ y me parece que sus precios son prohibitivos.

snippet:
Fuimos por curiosidad, porque todo el mundo tiene como referencia en Oporto al Majestic como el mejor café de la ciudad... y también el más caro.

Local antiguo, pero pudiendo estar mucho mejor conservado (había hasta humedad en los espejos).
Calidad del café muy justita, con azucarillos de poco gramaje, y aún encima ni bien calientes estaban.
Precio por 2 trozos de tarta, un capucchino y un café bombom: 28 euros.

Con esos precios yo tambien tengo a un mayordomo abriendo la puerta incluso haciendo el pino puente, lo difícil es encontrar clientes que te lo paguen.

En definitiva, como broma está muy bien, pero si buscas la foto de rigor, sácala desde fuera, que es gratis.

The place is old, but it could be much better maintained (there was even dampness on the mirrors). The coffee quality was very poor, with tiny sugar cubes, and they weren't even hot.

In short, it's a good joke, but if you want the obligatory photo, take it from outside—it's free."}

snippet:
The Cafe is one of the old grand Cafes.
The reason for my low rating is their prices.
For example, Americano coffee usually costs €2.  At this cafe, it cost €7 which is a €5 premium.
This is for a large room packed with tables.
Unless you are an influencer or a cafe pilgrim, you can just stop by to appreciate the design and continue to a more cozy cafe elsewhere

snippet:
Come for the views and ambience but don’t come for the food. Most pricey and small portion restaurant in Porto.

Tourist trap for sure and you can get better quality food at most restaurant. Food in general was good and I only had the Portuguese French toast which is one slice of small baguette deep fried and top with a few raisins and almonds and a sprinkle of cinnamon with a vanilla sauce. That was 8 euros.

Don’t bother coming here for the coffee as it looks unappealing.

Probably can check it out for once but order the cheapest item on the menu and take your photos/videos and get out of there.

Probably can check it out for once but order the cheapest item on the menu and take your photos/videos and get out of there.

snippet:
We were on a road trip in Portugal mid of June this year, Majestic Cafe was the first restaurant in the checklist, we came with very high expectations of how good the Portuguese food should be. It was definitely a famous hotspot in the central Porto and attracts a lot of tourists , but please think twice if your sole purpose is good food.

We came at around 3pm in the afternoon, the restaurant was quite busy, few people queue outside still. Again, think twice if you really want to queue for eating photos.

It is located in a historical building with beautiful European decoration and I believe that is why it attracts the tourists to do some instagram worthy pictures.

The seating arrangement was terrible, the space among each table was so narrow and tight, came in a group of four , we were told by the waiter to squeeze into a tiny table and two of us need sit in the pathway that waiters and other clients passing by all the time, it was really uncomfortable to enjoy in such a confined space. We requested the waiters to help us find another place, they were not very attentive until we asked multiple times.
The lesson learned was maybe you just drop by for a coffee and some light snack , take pictures and quickly leave , but definitely not to come for food.

The food itself is struggled at mediocre to average line, as you can see from the picture, we ordered various dishes plus two desserts, nothing was impressed, the French toast was way too sweet. That is level 100 sweet not in a pleasant way.

Last but not the least, this is probably the most expensive place we have been to in the entire Portugal, the food served in the picture cost us EUR152, for sure not very good value of money.

snippet:
The decor is the reason to come see this place. The food is definitely about double the normal price, which is what you'll be paying to sit in the decor.

Unfortunately, the food and drinks are not nearly up to par for the price. In fact, the nata was so below par that it was more than a waste of money.

There were no lines at about 20 minutes before opening, but lines are probably about 30 minutes even after about 20 minutes after opening. If you're going for the decor, be among the first to not waste the time.

snippet:
Just another overrated café—no taste, no service.

The café's architecture is as majestic as its prices, but I can't say the same for the taste or especially the staff. Their attire is great, but their attitude is quite off-putting. Most of them don’t speak English. Except Joel he is amazing. When we wanted to change tables, they insisted on speaking Portuguese to us, trying to explain something. When we finally changed tables, a waiter brushed past me with the back of his hand and said something in Portuguese without even looking at me, as if to say, "You forgot something here." Normally, I wouldn’t make a big deal of it, but if I’m paying 6 euros for an americano, I expect service to match.

Visualmente vivo, emocionalmente vacío.

snippet: O local é muito bonito, realmente remete todo luxo do século passado. Porém os preços são exorbitantes, beira a exploração!! E é um lanche normal sem nem um toque de chef que justifique o valor altíssimo, não tem um presunto Parma, uma burrata, um brie, um sabor trufado ou qualquer ingrediente que justifique tal preço! 60 euros, 1 café, 1 chá, 1 tosta (2 fatias de pão com manteiga), 1 croissant de queijo, 2 rabanadas e 2 pastéis de nata. Em qualquer lugar de qualidade e menos hypado custaria no máximo 30! E tb acho que não é pq o lugar é de 1920 que os espelhos tb precisariam ser, troquem esses espelhos velhos e oxidados façam me o favor. O atendimento poderia ser melhor. Enfim, não é um local que eu levaria outras pessoas que venham me visitar.

snippet:
Wunderschönes altes Café, welches hochgradig auf Tourismus kommerzialisiert wurde.
Das Ambiente ist wirklich toll und wohl auch noch im Originalzustand. Bei uns war es ziemlich voll, aber ist auch ein sehr beliebter Ort.
Der Service war ok. Die Kellnerin etwas wortkarg und nicht übermäßig freundlich. Aber kann man noch verzeihen bei dem vielen Touris.
Das Essen ist m.E. das Geld nicht wert. Ich habe noch nie einen so mittelmäßigen Burger für 22 Euro gegessen. Die Pommes sind sogar bei McDonalds besser. Tee kostet 6 Euro. Auch nicht wirklich günstig.
Fazit: schönes Café, aber viel zu überteuert.

The ambiance is truly wonderful and seems to be still in its original condition. It was quite crowded when we were there, but it's also a very popular spot.

snippet:
Bar storico e famoso che però si è preservato malissimo! Il bar è famoso per la scrittrice che scriveva sui tovaglioli che tra l’altro erano brutti e mal messi, il tavolo era pure mal pulito e con due bustine di zucchero buttate lì, i prezzi esorbitanti non  giustificano la noncuranza, per lo più un muro era rovinato e ci avevano attaccato con lo scotch da pacchi un filo della corrente! Bar storico? dovrebbe essere uno dei bar più belli e lussuriosi di Porto ma a me sembra che cerchino solo di approfittarne finché possono.
Il cibo era mediocre, molto meglio in altri posti, il servizio molto buono, i camerieri erano gentili e i bagni abbastanza puliti detto questo non consiglio assolutamente.

The bar is famous for the writer who wrote on the napkins, which were also ugly and shabby. The table was also poorly cleaned, with two sugar packets thrown away. The exorbitant prices don't justify the carelessness. A wall was mostly ruined, and they'd taped a power cord to it with packing tape! A historic bar? It should be one of the most beautiful and luxurious bars in Porto, but it seems to me they're just trying to take advantage of it while they can.\nThe food was mediocre, much better elsewhere. The service was very good, the waiters were friendly, and the bathrooms were fairly clean. That said, I definitely wouldn't recommend it."}

snippet:
Muy bonito por fuera y por dentro, pero como casi todos los lugares de Oporto, en temporada alta es impracticable.
7€ por un café simplón y 3€ por un pastelito de nata. No volveríamos a repetir. Además pedimos café frío y nos lo trajeron hirviendo jaja
Aceptan perros en el exterior.

snippet:
Je conseille pour un café, un goûter.
Sandwichs très chers, et petits.
Les plats ne sont malheureusement pas très bons…. Mais le service impeccable. Souriant, accueillant et gentil. C’est dommage qu’il n’y ait pas ces ingrédients dans la nourriture..

Mais le lieu est magnifique !

snippet:
El lugar es muy lindo. Si estás por Porto tenes que conocerlo ya que es una de las atracciones turísticas y típicas de acá.

Fuimos a la hora de la merienda, por un café y tostado, normal. El café es caro (5€) para lo que es, no es nada wow y es típico café delta que en otros lugares sale 1,70€ como máximo.

3 cafés con leche, un tostado con queso y uno mixto costó 40€🫣

No descarto la posibilidad de volver para probar la comida.

snippet:
可以經過拍照就好，完全不需要入內用餐，因為價格跟味道都會讓人很疑惑很安靜⋯⋯

空間很大，但因為客人多所以非常擁擠，服務生動作滿快的，會注意客人。

咖啡香氣滿夠濃度也不錯，不過已經完全超出葡萄牙超出波多的價格，喝氣氛的。

French toast 法國吐司，原本是看別人推薦而點的，但吃完眉頭深鎖的到不行，很油膩不太確定真的是油還是醬料，醬料也吃不出所以然，整個麵包像雙胞胎但雙胞胎比較好吃。

必須來的原因只是因為知名度，但其實來波多不到訪也完全不覺得可惜。

必須來的原因只是因為知名度，但其實來波多不到訪也完全不覺得可惜。', 'translated': "You can just take photos; there's absolutely no need to go inside to dine. The prices and taste are quite questionable, and it's very quiet…

The space is large, but it's very crowded because of the many customers. The servers are quite quick and attentive to their guests.

The coffee has a good aroma and a good strength, but it's priced way above the average for Portugal and Porto. It's more about the atmosphere.

The French toast was ordered based on recommendations, but I was incredibly disappointed after eating it. It was very oily, and I wasn't sure if it was oil or sauce. I couldn't even taste the sauce. The whole bread looked like twins, but the twins were tastier.

The only reason I had to come was because of its fame, but honestly, if you come to Porto and don't visit, you won't regret it at all."}

snippet:
Lugar hermoso, con historia y valor especial por ello, aunque en buenos aires he conocido similares, y mejor mantenidos.
Los precios son muy elevados una coca cola o un café 5 euros, unas tapas con pan frío 22 euros.
Si eres turista y quieres conocer algo hermoso está bien, pero no es un lugar para volver a visitar.

snippet:
Niente di che, servizio e qualità scarsi e quindi prezzi esagerati non giustificati soltanto dal nome: 5 € per un caffè; nei bar "normali" con questa cifra si
prendono 5-6 caffè. Mi sembra che il suo richiamo sia dovuto più ad una abile operazione di marketing che ad una vera
attrattiva dovuta all'ambientazione antica. Nemmeno che abbia chissà quale vista: affaccia su Rua Santa Catarina e guardando fuori si vede soltanto la gente in coda per entrare e i gruppi di
curiosi che scrutano all'interno.

snippet:
Место с красивым интерьером и на этом всё. Брали кофе капучино и апероль. Цены выше, чем в других местах и при этом всё это ниже среднего. Апероль был с какими-то черными точками в стакане, а капучино молочное-молоко, которое чуть лучше, чем из кофейного аппарата на заправке. При этом кофе подали в разбитой чашке. Крайне не советую данное место. Будете стоять в очереди и ждать непонятно чего. Лучше пойти в менее популярное место и получить больше приятных впечатлений.
Трата времени и денег. Проходите мимо

snippet: Café com charme de época más que derrapa na qualidade dos produtos e serviço. Local para ser visitado somente para cumprir tabela. Fui com a recomendação de comer a rabanada com doce de ovos acompanhada de uma taça de vinho do Porto, que custaram 10.00€. A rabanada estava fria, achatada e encharcada com uma calda de açúcar muito doce, que em nada lembrava um doce de ovos. Foi uma tremenda decepção para mim. O serviço é desatento parecendo mais um fast food. O rapaz que organizava a fila na porta no dia que fui, não era nada simpático, e o atendimento de mesa não compatível com os preços cobrados. Vá  sem expectativas e tenha mais sorte que eu.

Si van a poner estos precios por lo menos la limpieza tendría que ser de 10… por lo demás todo normal dejo aquí las fotos de todo un poco! Es local ya podéis apreciar…

snippet: Esta bien si quieres ir por sacarte alguna foto tomando algo y verlo por dentro pero los precios por los dulces y las bebidas que ofrecen son excesivamente altos con respecto a su calidad. Pedimos tres cafés, dos pasteles de Belém y una porción de tarta de queso, los pasteles estaban buenos, los cafés sin más no me parecieron mejor ni peor que los que he probado en otras cafeterías más corrientes. El ambiente por la mañana y a media tarde es también un poco agobiante ya que hay mucha gente y muy poca distancia entre mesa y mesa al punto de que los propios camareros suelen tener que pedir que muevas un poco tu silla para poder pasar, el precio por los tres cafés y los bollos fue de unos 34 euros... A mi parecer la gente que llega al local lo hace más por obtener un par de imágenes para subir a las redes sociales que por la calidad de lo que se ofrece

snippet:
오래되고도 유명한 포르투의 시그니쳐 같은 카페 입니다.
아주 화려하면서도 앤틱 빈티지 뭐 그런 느낌입니다.
오픈시간에 맞춰 가지 않으면 대기하셔야 합니다.
구글에는 9:30에 연다고 되어있는데 더 일찍 여는것 같습니다.

문앞 직원이 앉으라는 테이블에 앉으면 전담 직원이 주문을 받는데요 전담직원이 아니면 주문을 할수 없는 시스템 입니다. 적잖히 당황했는데요 마냥 기다렸습니다.

커피 몇가지랑 프렌치토스트, 나타 등 기본적으로 주문했는데요 맛은 없습니다.
차라리 공항에 있는 프렌차이즈가 낫습니다.

겉멋만 잔뜩 들어서 이름값으로 관광덕에 먹고사는 가게 라는 생각 입니다.
그 겉멋이 굉장해서 어쩔수 없이 가야되는 가게인가 하는 생각이 들더군요.

친절하지도 않고 팁은 단 한푼도 주기 싫은 세계적인 카페 입니다.

친절하지도 않고 팁은 단 한푼도 주기 싫은 세계적인 카페 입니다.', 'translated': "This is a long-established and famous café, a signature of Porto.
It's a very ornate, antique-vintage feel.
If you don't go at opening time, you'll have to wait.
Google says it opens at 9:30, but it seems to open earlier.

The staff at the door tells you to sit at a table, and a dedicated staff member takes your order. It's a system where you can't order unless you're a dedicated staff member. I was quite perplexed, but I just waited.

I ordered some basics like coffee, French toast, and nata, but they were all tasteless.
I'd rather go to a franchise at the airport.

It's a place that's all show, trying to live off the tourist attraction.
Their pretense is so great that I felt like I had to go.

This is a world-class café, unfriendly and unwilling to tip."}

Je ne qualifierais pas cette brasserie comme l’une des plus belle du monde. D’autres lieux plus emblématiques de porto sont à faire. Je déconseille ce détour. Autre chose on vous fait comprendre après 15minutes que vous devez partir…

snippet:
Sehr tolles Ambiente und der Chef ist vom alten Schlag. Sehr angenehm. Dieser sollte seinem Personal mehr hinterher sein.Woanders schmeckt aber der Kaffee deutlich besser. Verstehe dadurch den Hype nicht.Als Pilger wenn man nur ein Kaffee trinken will darf man auch rein…nur beim bezahlen und wenig Trinkgeld wird man beim Restgeld übers Ohr gehauen. Eigentlich schade.
Man muss nicht immer etwas essen um die Geschichte in Zettelform zu bekommen.Aber es wird unterschieden zwischen Kaffeetrinker und die die essen.
Somit lieber außerhalb der Touristenzone was essen und trinken gehen und man bekommt egal wieviel Geld man liegen lässt mehr Respekt als leider hier…schade

snippet: Normalmente tem fila, mas andou bem rápido. O lugar é bonito, me lembrou muito a confeitaria Colombo do Rio de Janeiro. O atendimento é ruim, a comida não é aquelas coisas e a porção é pequena. Pedi uma torta de maçã que estava de razoável pra ruim e era minúscula. Preço pra turista. Nem o cappuccino salvou. Eles servem rápido pq querem que a rotatividade de clientes seja grande. Você paga pela experiência, que não é tudo isso. Acho que não vale a pena a espera na fila. Pra não dizer que tudo tava ruim, o chocolate quente tava gostoso.

snippet:
Español:

El Café Majestic de Oporto es un sitio que, aunque tiene una atmósfera espectacular y una arquitectura imponente, resulta grotescamente caro para lo que ofrece. Si bien la decoración es digna de admiración y te hace retroceder en el tiempo, el servicio deja mucho que desear. La atención es lenta y parece que el personal se enfoca más en la cantidad de turistas que en ofrecer una experiencia de calidad. En definitiva, es un lugar que se aprovecha de su reputación y su historia, pero no está a la altura de lo que se esperaría de un sitio con esos precios.

English:

The Café Majestic in Porto, while boasting an impressive atmosphere and stunning architecture, is grotesquely overpriced for what it delivers. Although the decor is something to admire, transporting you back in time, the service is subpar. The staff seems more focused on the volume of tourists than on providing a quality experience, and the attention is notably slow. Ultimately, it’s a place that leans heavily on its reputation and history but falls short of the standards one would expect at such prices.

snippet:
キラキラした口コミを見て入店しました。
確かに歴史的に有名な内装は素晴らしいかったし、味も良かったですが、コストパフォーマンスはあまり納得できませんでした。フレンチトーストは確かにおいしいですが、1切れのフランスパンに10ユーロ。海鮮パスタも大きなエビが入っていました。2人でシェアしたためにあらかじめ取り分けてくれたのはよかったです（写真は取り分けた分の量)しかし39ユーロは明らかに高すぎ。
価格表とか見ないで食べたいものを注文できる人はいいですが、私みたいな一般庶民にはコスパがかなり悪いと感じました。ウェイターのサービスや態度も中の下ぐらいだった。

It was nice that they portioned it out for us since we were sharing (the photo shows the portion we shared), but 39 euros is clearly too expensive.

snippet: There’s a table sheet you get which states how majestic the Majestic Cafe was in 1922. Unfortunately, that is now a misnomer. Had I had the same service, dry cake and a small coffee in a bad station cafe for about €7, I might have felt it was reasonable. But, here, for €37 it was a complete waste of money. My friend said we were paying for ‘the atmosphere’, but that too was sadly lacking and the staff seemed stressed and not too keen on each other or their profession. I wouldn’t t bother if I were you!

snippet: Francesinha!!! Order it. I honestly would leave the review at just that because of how delicious it was. Truthfully I was on the fence as to whether to order it or not because it looked very heavy to be my breakfast (we arrived brunch time). There was a line out the door of about 15-20ish people, but worth the wait and the line does move fairly quickly from our experience. It is no surprise once your seated inside, because we were able to see with the rapid quickness that servers zoom around and bus and wait on people. However, we never once felt rushed. Also, they ask what language you speak before seating you and put you in a section based on that. Absolutely brillant, in my humble opinion. We again were hemming and hawing at what to order and then saw the fracesinha brought out to the table next to us, and well we couldn’t resist. My cousin and I split it, and it really was the right call for us, because we wanted to save room for their pastel de nata, which also did not disappoint. The gravy or sauce on the francesinha was delectable, we kept dunking our fries in it. The pastel de nata was light and toasted. There is no wonder this is a spot that is high in demand. Not only is the food and service spot on, but the history and decor of the building and restaurant also make it a must visit in Porto.
